name: github_corpus_convergence
host_notes: Phase D follow-up to phase_c_per_query_budget_null_result.json. Where the per-query budget was a null result, phrase-based seed extraction is the actual chart improvement: round_success_rate 0.58 -> 0.83 on the same corpus, same scenario knobs, same provider, same cost.

code_change_under_test.summary: Index unique title tokens (>=8 chars) and unique 2-3 word phrases (>=12/18 chars, neither word a stopword) per PR. Drops the previous bare-number indexing for PRs <10 because single-digit ids generated false-positive seeds when intents contained incidental digits like 'scenario 5.1, 5.2, 5.3'. Bigrams also get a -s plural variant on the first word so 'scenarios 5.1' matches a title's 'scenario 5.1'.

skills_unblocked.adr_landing: 0.0 -> 1.0 — GraphSearch regression fixed; correct seeds now route GraphSearch to #3/#4/#5 directly instead of letting it over-fetch from generic neighbors
skills_unblocked.bug_fix_identification: 0.625 -> 1.0 — better seeds (e.g. 'StoreRegistry' as a unique title token -> #33) replace cosine-only ranking
skills_unblocked.multi_pr_series: 0.0 -> 0.4706 — Q2 (Neo4j hardening) now succeeds because 'driver lifecycle' / 'Phase 2.3' / 'Phase 1.3' / 'Phase 2.1' phrases anchor the right PRs

skills_unchanged.author_attribution: 0.0 -> 0.0 — needs filter-by-edge support in SeededGraphSearch (filter PRs by their wasAttributedTo edge to the dependabot user); separate from seed extraction

interpretation.headline: Phrase-based seed extraction is the chart improvement that the per-query pack budget hypothesis (Phase C) was searching for. The bottleneck wasn't pack space — it was that the seed extractor couldn't anchor intents that named no PR by number. Once unique title phrases and tokens enter the index, intents like 'ADRs for terminology, tag vocabulary, and storage planes' resolve to PR #3/#4/#5 directly, and GraphSearch's traversal lands the right pack.
interpretation.tightening_was_load_bearing: First implementation indexed 2-3 word phrases with >=2-char tokens and either-stopword filter. Got round_success_rate to 0.74 BUT regressed topic_content to 0.667. Generic phrases like 'bulk upsert' (in PR #41's title 'Consolidate Neo4j bulk upsert round trips') anchored seeds away from the correct introducing PR (#34). Tightening: token min-length 8, bigram min-length 12 (neither word stopword), trigram min-length 18 (first AND last non-stopword). Index size dropped 1961 -> 1154. round_success_rate climbed 0.74 -> 0.83 — keeping just the high-specificity phrases.

remaining_failures_categorized.multi_pr_series Q1 (eval-framework Phase: Intent has no entity-anchoring phrase; required PRs (#29-#32) titled 'Eval Phase N' but 'Phase 1', 'Phase 2', etc. are common across many PR titles -> not unique -> not indexed. Would need either body-token indexing (more aggressive) or LLM-based intent rewriting.
remaining_failures_categorized.author_attribution: Filter-by-edge intent ('PRs authored by app/dependabot, not by ronsse'). Seed extraction works (extracts user entity_ids) but the SeededGraphSearch wrapper has no notion of 'find PRs that have wasAttributedTo edge to this user'. Needs filter expansion in the strategy.

decision_recorded: Phrase-seed extraction commits as a real win. Author-attribution and multi_pr_series Q1 remain as scoped follow-ups; both have known mechanisms (edge-filter for author, body-token indexing or query rewriting for Q1) but neither is required for the headline result.
